pr.title: Update function names used in HSD_InitComponent
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Renamed several previously anonymous assembly labels to meaningful HSD/VI initialization-related symbols and updated their call sites, especially the functions invoked by HSD_InitComponent. The PR was a small naming-only assembly cleanup across initialize, video, text, and id assembly files, improving readability and establishing recognizable SDK-style names for core startup routines.
what_changed: In asm/initialize.s, HSD_InitComponent call sites were updated from anonymous func_* labels to named startup routines: HSD_VIInit, HSD_GXInit, HSD_DVDInit, HSD_IDSetup, VIWaitForRetrace, and HSD_ObjInit.

In asm/initialize.s, function definitions were renamed from func_80374F60 to HSD_GXSetFifoObj, func_80374F78 to HSD_DVDInit, func_80374F7C to HSD_AllocateXFB, func_80375258 to HSD_GXInit, and func_803755B4 to HSD_ObjInit.
In asm/id.s, func_8037CDBC was renamed to HSD_IDSetup.
In asm/video.s, func_803767B8 was renamed to HSD_VIInit, and existing calls to func_8034F314 were updated to VIWaitForRetrace.

In asm/text.s, func_8034F314 was renamed to VIWaitForRetrace and several call sites were updated; call sites to func_80374F7C and func_80374F60 were updated to HSD_AllocateXFB and HSD_GXSetFifoObj respectively.
The diff shows only symbol/global label and branch target name changes, with equal added/deleted line counts and no apparent instruction or control-flow changes.
smart_moves: Renamed the callee definitions and all visible branch/call sites together, avoiding mismatched labels after assigning meaningful names.
Used names that describe the role of routines in HSD_InitComponent rather than keeping address-derived func_* labels, making startup flow easier to audit.
Applied VIWaitForRetrace consistently outside HSD_InitComponent as well, replacing func_8034F314 at multiple unrelated wait/retrace call sites in text.s and video.s.
Preserved exact assembly instructions and addresses while changing only symbol names, minimizing matching risk.
decomp_lessons: When a top-level initializer such as HSD_InitComponent calls a sequence of unknown functions, naming the entire sequence can clarify subsystem initialization order before full C decompilation.
Propagate a newly identified symbol name globally across assembly call sites, not just at the defining label, so future searches and cross-references converge on one canonical name.
Address-derived labels should be replaced with subsystem-prefixed names once behavior is known or strongly inferred, but the PR evidence does not explain the external source used for these names.
Naming-only PRs can still be valuable in early decomp work because they improve call graph readability without changing binary behavior.
naming_conventions: HSD subsystem routines use the HSD_ prefix with PascalCase-style component/action names, e.g. HSD_InitComponent, HSD_VIInit, HSD_GXInit, HSD_DVDInit, HSD_IDSetup, HSD_ObjInit, HSD_AllocateXFB, HSD_GXSetFifoObj.
The VI retrace wrapper/function was named VIWaitForRetrace without an HSD_ prefix, matching the apparent external VI-style naming rather than the HSD subsystem prefix.
Anonymous labels followed the func_XXXXXXXX address pattern and were replaced only when a clearer semantic name was available.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Performed direct assembly symbol renames by changing both `.global` declarations and the following label definitions.
Updated `bl` branch-and-link targets to the new names while keeping instruction bytes and addresses unchanged.
Kept the change binary-neutral: all diff hunks are textual symbol substitutions in assembly comments/labels/call targets, not instruction rewrites.
Used initializer call order as evidence for naming related routines: VI init, GX init, DVD init, ID setup, retrace wait, and object init.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that move HSD_VIInit, HSD_GXInit, HSD_DVDInit, HSD_IDSetup, HSD_ObjInit, HSD_AllocateXFB, or HSD_GXSetFifoObj from asm into C.
Search for later discussion or corrections around whether VIWaitForRetrace is the final canonical name for func_8034F314.
Check whether these HSD_* names came from SDK symbols, debug strings, map files, or inference in later documentation.
